query: I have a brand-new NestJS app and want to add nest-profiler. Where do we start?
expected_behavior: Must start by introspecting the project (package manager from the lockfile, package.json dependencies, the composition root and main.ts, whether @nestjs/config is present) before writing any code. Must NOT jump straight to pasting a ProfilerModule snippet.

End-to-end setup. Choose Approach A (recommended, @nestjs/config present). Install with pnpm, pinning the profiler to the alpha dist-tag (no stable release yet): 'pnpm add @eleven-labs/nest-profiler@alpha nestjs-cls'. Detect TypeORM and offer the nest-profiler-typeorm collector (TypeOrmCollectorModule, optionally the TypeOrmSchemaCollectorModule panel) placed after TypeOrmModule and gated the same way — NO inject: [DataSource] (it self-resolves the DataSource via connectionName). Add the logger wrap in main.ts (bufferLogs + the standalone createProfilerLogger, no ProfilerService injection). Add PROFILER_ENABLED=true to .env for dev (code default is off). Verify via curl for X-Debug-Token and /_profiler.

query: My app already has ProfilerModule wired up and I just added @nestjs/cache-manager. How do I add cache profiling?
expected_behavior: Recognize the core profiler is already configured and this is adding a single collector — route the user to the add-nest-profiler-collector skill rather than running a full setup.

Recognize this is NOT a NestJS application (no @nestjs/core; plain Express). nest-profiler targets NestJS 11+ on Node 22+, so it does not apply. Should not wire ProfilerModule; instead explain the requirement and ask the user to confirm it is a NestJS app.

Introspect and detect multiple relevant collectors: nest-profiler-cache (hard peer @nestjs/cache-manager) and nest-profiler-auth (heuristic via @nestjs/passport/@nestjs/jwt). Must ASK the user (multi-select) which detected collectors to add rather than assuming all. Wire core + logger, place globals at root and infra collectors co-located, and recommend keeping the profiler off in production by default (without stating it as an absolute ban).

query: I'm wiring log capture in main.ts for the profiler. What exactly goes in there?
expected_behavior: Create the app with { bufferLogs: true }, then app.useLogger(createProfilerLogger(new ConsoleLogger('App'))) — importing the standalone createProfilerLogger from @eleven-labs/nest-profiler. Must NOT resolve ProfilerService for logging (no app.get(ProfilerService), no logger method on ProfilerService): createProfilerLogger is DI-free (reads the active profile from CLS) and is a transparent pass-through when the profiler is off, so it needs no ProfilerService and no ProfilerNoopModule. A directly-injected logger (e.g. PinoLogger) is wrapped the same way at its injection point with createProfilerLogger(pinoLogger).

query: package.json includes @nestjs/common, @nestjs/core and @nestjs/config. User: My API is internal only, reachable through our VPN, and I understand the exposure. I want the profiler enabled in production too, not just in dev.
expected_behavior: Must NOT flatly refuse. Respect the informed explicit choice (internal API behind a VPN, risks understood) while noting it is off-by-default for good reason. Help them harden it per references/core-options.md (or route to the harden-for-production skill): the profiler is open by default so provide a `security` strategy (reuse an app guard via `security.guards`, or an `authorize` predicate) for /_profiler, keep collectBody: false, lower sampleRate and cap maxProfiles/ttl, add ignorePaths/ignoreRequest for sensitive routes, and mind file/sqlite persistence. Can still use PROFILER_ENABLED so it stays toggleable. Must NOT reference a built-in token/PROFILER_TOKEN (removed).

Choose Approach C (devDependency-only dev-entry split). Install with the dev flag: 'pnpm add -D @eleven-labs/nest-profiler@alpha nestjs-cls'. Keep production main.ts + AppModule profiler-free (plain ConsoleLogger + ValidationPipe); create a dev-only AppDevModule (imports AppModule + the profiler bundle) imported only by main-dev.ts, which wires createProfilerLogger + createProfilerValidationPipe. No ConditionalModule/@nestjs/config, no PROFILER_ENABLED gate, no ProfilerNoopModule, no env-condition helpers. Add scripts (start:dev via --entryFile main-dev; start = node dist/main.js). Verify BOTH: dev entry boots with /_profiler, and the production build boots with the profiler pruned (no MODULE_NOT_FOUND, /_profiler 404).
